revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]]
[[File:いたち川.jpg|thumb|250px|Itachigawa River (Photographed in September 2017)]] [[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-1.jpg|thumb|250px|One of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa River Basin Yokoanabo Groups|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa River Basin Yokoanabo Groups|qq=}}, the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97613655}} (located in {{ill|Kajigaya, Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigaya, Sakae-ku, Yokohama|1=Kajigaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11650738}}, {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qid=Q1153916|lt=Sakae-ku, Yokohama|1=Sakae-ku, Yokohama}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|qid=Q38283|lt=Yokohama|1=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}})]]
'''Itachigawa River Basin Yokoanabo Groups''' (㹨川流域横穴墓群) refers to a collection of ancient {{ill|Yokoana tomb group|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|lt=Yokoanabos|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tomb group|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} sites that once existed in the mountains surrounding the {{ill|Itachigawa River|ja|㹨川|1=Itachi River|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa River|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11259475}}, which flows through {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qid=Q1153916|lt=Sakae-ku, Yokohama|1=Sakae-ku, Yokohama}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|qid=Q38283|lt=Yokohama|1=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. These tombs are characterized by their distinctive "{{ill|Kamakura-style cave tombs|lt=Yokoanabos|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tombs|lt=Yokoanabos|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|Kamakura-style cave tombs=Kamakura-style cave tombs|lt=Kajigaya-style Yokoanabos|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓}}), but only a few, such as the {{ill|Shichisekiyama Yokoanabo Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}}, remain today.
== Overview == Between the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} and the early {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, many cave-like tombs known as {{ill|Yokoana tomb groups|ja|横穴墓群|1=Yokoanabo|lt=Yokoanabos|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tomb groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横穴墓}} were carved into the slopes of mountains in Kanagawa Prefecture. The region surrounding the {{ill|Itachigawa River|ja|㹨川|1=Itachi River|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa River|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11259475}}, which forms a mountainous boundary between {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|qid=Q38283|lt=Yokohama|1=Yokohama}} and {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|qid=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}}, was no exception, with numerous burial sites found along these mountain slopes.
A defining characteristic of these tombs is the presence of a unique shelf-like structure known as a "kan-shitsu" (棺室) within the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|lt=Genshitsu|lt=Gen-shitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gen-shitsu|qq=}}), giving rise to the distinctive "{{ill|Kamakura-style cave tombs|lt=Yokoanabos|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" classification.<ref>Sakae Ward Community Promotion Division, ''History of Sakae'' (2013), pp. 20–21.</ref><ref>Yokohama City Buried Cultural Property Center, ''Important Archaeological Sites of Sakae Ward'' (2015), pp. 20–30.</ref>
At its peak, the Itachigawa River Basin contained approximately 20 clusters comprising over 200 individual tombs, though not all of them conformed to the "Kamakura-style."<ref>Yokohama City Buried Cultural Property Center, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1–2.</ref>
== Tomb groups == {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration)
* {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}} , Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Yokoanabo Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}} , Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally * {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs * {{ill|Hikoshigaya Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs == Examples of Itachigawa River Basin Yokoanabo Groups == * Iijimacho Tono Valley Yokoanabo Group * Takigakubo Yokoanabo Group * Sekiyama Yokoanabo Group * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Yokoanabo Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} * Former Hongo Elementary School Back Yokoanabo Group * Katsura Town B Yokoanabo Group * {{ill|Shōōji Cemetery Back Yokoanabo Group|ja|正翁寺墓地裏横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōōji Cemetery Back Yokoanabo Group|1=Cave Tombs behind Shooji Temple Cemetery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q113482167}} * {{ill|Nakai Maruyama Yokoanabo Group|ja|中居丸山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakai Maruyama Yokoanabo Group|1=Nakai Maruyama Cave Tomb Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q114045023}} * {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97613655}} * Higashitani Yokoanabo Group * Ōhashidani Yokoanabo Group * Kamihongo Abuna Valley Yokoanabo Group * {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoanabo Cluster|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Located in {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qid=Q1153968|lt=Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|1=Kōnan-ku, Yokohama}}) * {{ill|Hikoshiga Valley Yokoanabo Group|ja|ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshiga Valley Yokoanabo Group|1=Hikoshigaya Yokoanabo Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97636128}}
While some of these tomb groups no longer exist, the {{ill|Shichisekiyama Yokoanabo Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97613655}}, {{ill|Nakai Maruyama Yokoanabo Group|ja|中居丸山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakai Maruyama Yokoanabo Group|1=Nakai Maruyama Cave Tomb Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q114045023}}, {{ill|Shōōji Cemetery Back Yokoanabo Group|ja|正翁寺墓地裏横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōōji Cemetery Back Yokoanabo Group|1=Cave Tombs behind Shooji Temple Cemetery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q113482167}}, {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoanabo Cluster|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hikoshiga Valley Yokoanabo Group|ja|ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshiga Valley Yokoanabo Group|1=Hikoshigaya Yokoanabo Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97636128}} still remain today.
== Reference Books == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11675787}} Geography & History Club, ''On the Yokoanabos of the Itachigawa River Basin in Yokohama City,'' March 20, 1964.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Sakae Ward History Editing Committee, ''History of Sakae,'' Sakae Ward Community Promotion Division, March 2013. * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Property Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Cultural Property Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30929301}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31: Yokoanabos of the Itachigawa River Basin],'' March 20, 2015. * Yokohama City Buried Cultural Property Center, ''Important Archaeological Sites of Sakae Ward,'' November 13, 2015.
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Kanagawa Prefecture}}
== See Also == * **
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoanabos|en|Kashiya Yokoanabos|qid=Q22118267|lt=Kashiya Yokoanabos|1=Kashiya Yokoanabos}} ({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoanabos|en|Hōōzan Yokoanabos|qid=Q18339249|lt=Hōōzan Yokoanabos|1=Hōōzan Cave Tombs}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga, Ishikawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoanabos|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Yokoanabos|qq=}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfukuji Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūōmen Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunedani Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikonishi Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuku Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu, Shimane|qq=}}, [[:en:Oki District|Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenami Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukuhashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchi, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizumachi Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōgata|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoanabo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozuki Yokoanabo|qq=}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Nagino Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tamana|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamana|qq=}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabeta Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga, Kumamoto|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmura Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoyoshi|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster|en|Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster|qid=Q11419962|lt=Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster|1=Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster}} ({{ill|Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoanabo Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Yokoanabo Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki, Miyazaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokoanabos.
*
